Ayahs

ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ (۱)
The All-beneficent (1)
عَلَّمَ ٱلْقُرْءَانَ (۲)
has taught the Quran. (2)

Key Points

Allah is Rahman (The Most Compassionate). "الرّحمن" His Prophet is a source of mercy. "و ما ارسلناك الاّ رحمة للعالمین" (2) His Quran is also mercy. "و رحمة للمؤمنین" (3) Rahman is a special Name of Allah, mentioned 169 times in the Quran and repeated 114 times in every Bismillāh. In some instances in the Quran, the word "الرّحمن" has been used instead of "اللّه": «قالوا اتّخذ اللّه ولدا» (4); «قالوا اتّخذ الرّحمن ولدا» (5) «اتاكم عذاب اللّه» (6); «یَمسّك عذاب من الرّحمن» (7) «قل ادعوا اللّه او ادعوا الرّحمن» (8) In the art of rhetoric and writing, the opening of the discourse is a sign and manifestation of the entire speech. Here too, since the Surah looks at divine blessings, "الرّحمن" has been placed at its beginning. The Quran must be the axis of all affairs. In the previous Surah (Al-Qamar), the ease of the Quran was mentioned repeatedly: "لقد یسّرنا القرآن للذّكر" In this Surah, Allah is introduced as its first Teacher. "علم القرآن" Knowledge is a manifestation of mercy when it is taught by Allah. "الرّحمن علّم" Knowledge is a manifestation of mercy when it is comprehensive. "تبیاناً لكلّ شى ء" (9) Knowledge is a manifestation of mercy when it seeks no reward from us and is based on mercy. "الرّحمن علّم" Knowledge is a manifestation of mercy when it is applicable in every era. Knowledge is a manifestation of mercy when it creates insight and piety. Knowledge is a manifestation of mercy when it secures the happiness of the individual and society, both in this world and the Hereafter. The teaching of the Quran by Allah, "علّم القرآن", means: Teaching the truth. "نزل علیك الكتاب بالحق" (10) Teaching judgment. "أنزلنا الیك الكتاب بالحقّ لتحكم بین النّاس" (11) Teaching what cannot be learned through ordinary means. "و علّمك مالم تكن تعلم" (12) Teaching proof/evidence. "قد جاءكم برهان" (13) Teaching righteous deeds. "و الّذین یمسكون بالكتاب... انا لانضیع أجر المصلحین" (14) Teaching admonition, healing for the hearts, guidance, and mercy. "قد جائتكم موعظة من ربّكم و شفاء لما فى الصّدور و هدىً و رحمة للمؤمنین" (15) Teaching the unseen. "ذلك من أنباء الغیب" (16) Teaching the best stories. "نحن نقصّ علیك أحسن القصص" (17) Teaching the recognition of realities. "تبیاناً لكلّ شى ء" (18) Teaching the best things. "و لایأتونك بمثل الاّ جئناك بالحقّ و أحسن تفسیراً" (19) Teaching the standard for recognizing truth from falsehood. "أنزل الكتاب بالحقّ و المیزان" (20) Teaching the soundest speech. "لایأتیه الباطل من بین یدیه و لا من خلفه" (21) Teaching admonition. "فذكّر بالقرآن مَن یخاف وعید" (22) Teaching maturity/right guidance. "یهدى الى الرّشد" (23) Teaching a Book whose preservation is guaranteed. "انّا نحن نزّلنا الذكر و انّا له لحافظون" (24) The source of the Quran: Power and Greatness: "تنزیلاً ممن خلق الارض و السّموات العلى" (25) Might and Wisdom: "تنزیلُ الكتاب من اللّه العزیز الحكیم" (26) Grace and Mercy: "تنزیلٌ من الرّحمن الرحیم" (27) Lordship and Nurturing: "تنزیلٌ من ربّ العالمین" (28)

2) Al-Anbiya, 107. 3) Al-Isra, 82. 4) Al-Baqarah, 116. 5) Maryam, 88. 6) Al-An'am, 47. 7) Maryam, 45. 8) Al-Isra, 110. 9) An-Nahl, 89. 10) Al-i Imran, 3. 11) An-Nisa, 105. 12) An-Nisa, 113. 13) An-Nisa, 174. 14) Al-A'raf, 170. 15) Yunus, 57. 16) Yusuf, 102. 17) Yusuf, 3. 18) An-Nahl, 89. 19) Al-Furqan, 33. 20) Ash-Shura, 17. 21) Fussilat, 42. 22) Qaf, 45. 23) Al-Jinn, 2. 24) Al-Hijr, 9. 25) Taha, 4. 26) Az-Zumar, 1. 27) Fussilat, 2. 28) Al-Waqi'ah, 80.

Messages

  1. Allah is the first Teacher of the Quran. "الرّحمن علّم القرآن"
  2. The Divine Law (Shari'ah) is based on mercy. "الرّحمن علّم القرآن"
  3. Doubts must be answered. In response to those who say that a human taught the Quran to the Prophet: "یعلّمه بشر" (29), it must be said: Allah taught it. "الرّحمن علّم القرآن"
  4. The teaching of the Quran to humanity is a manifestation of Divine mercy. "الرّحمن علّم القرآن"
  5. Teaching is the status of Allah. "الرّحمن علّم القرآن"
  6. The act of teaching and learning must be based on love and mercy. "الرّحمن علّم القرآن"
  7. The Quran is comprehensible and knowable by humanity. "علّم القرآن" 29) An-Nahl, 103.

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Ar-Rahman

This Surah has seventy-eight verses and was revealed in Medina. The name of the Surah is taken from the first verse, and "الرّحمن," like "اللّه," is among the special Names and Attributes of Allah. Imam Kadhim عليه السلام said: Everything has a bride, and the bride of the Quran is Surah Ar-Rahman. (1) In this Surah, the verse "فباىّ آلاء ربّكما تكذّبان" is repeated thirty-three times, which is the most frequent repetition in the entire Quran. It is interesting that this repeated verse addresses both humans and jinn, asking which of the countless divine blessings they deny. In the previous Surah, the warning was repeated: "عذابى و نذر" (My punishment and My warnings); in this Surah, the blessings are repeated. "فباىّ آلاء ربّكما..." The previous Surah began with the Hour (of Judgment) and ended with Divine power: "اقتربت الساعة... ملیك مقتدر." But this Surah begins with mercy and concludes with Divine honor. "الرّحمن... ذى الجلال و الاكرام"

1) Wasā’il, Vol. 4, p. 351.

Ayah

خَلَقَ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ (۳)
He created man, (3)

Key Points

The creation of man is noteworthy from various perspectives: From the perspective of the developmental path, how a living, conscious being emerges from lifeless dust. From the perspective of securing material needs, how the world of nature has been subjugated for him. From the perspective of positive and negative inclinations, some of which the Quran has pointed out, including: He quickly despairs. «قتوراً»(30) He is greedy. «هلوع»(31) He is impatient. «جزوعاً»(32) He is a loser (or in loss). «لفى خسر»(33) He is excessive (or transgresses). «لیطغى »(34) He is oppressive. «ظلوماً»(35) He is ignorant. «جهولاً»(36) He is hasty. «عجولا»(37) He is ungrateful. «كفور»(38) He is unappreciative (or thankless). «كنود»(39)

Other verses of the Quran present the positive characteristics of man as follows: acceptance of responsibility, possessing freedom and the power of choice; the possibility of changing methods, repentance (tawbah), and internal revolution and leap; possessing great inner talents and capacities; the characteristic of seeking the infinite; the vicegerent of Allah on earth; the one prostrated to by the angels; possessing the power of imitation and being influenced, or the capacity to become a role model and exert influence; a composition of contradictions—intellect (aql) and innate nature (fitrah) versus instinct and lust; possessing the power of thought, creativity, and innovation.

Where the discussion is about teaching the Quran, it precedes the creation of man. «علّم القرآن خلق الانسان» But where the teaching of other sciences is discussed, the creation of man precedes it. «خلق الانسان من علق.... الّذى علّم بالقلم»(40)

30) Al-Isra, 100. 31) Al-Ma'arij, 19. 32) Al-Ma'arij, 20. 33) Al-Asr, 2. 34) Al-Alaq, 6. 35) Al-Ahzab, 72. 36) Al-Ahzab, 72. 37) Al-Isra, 11. 38) Al-Isra, 67. 39) Al-Adiyat, 6. 40) Al-Alaq, 2 - 4.

Messages

1- Allah is the Creator of man, not lifeless nature. «الرّحمن... خلق الانسان»

2- The creation of man is a manifestation of Divine Mercy. «الرّحمن... خلق الانسان»

3- The Author of the Book of Legislation (Tashrī‘) is the same Author of the Book of Creation (Takwīn). «علّم القرآن، خلق الانسان»

4- The Quran has superiority and precedence over man. (Thought and knowledge precede the physical body and material nature.) «علم القرآن، خلق الانسان»

5- The Quran is the foremost of all books, and man is the foremost of all creatures. «علّم القرآن، خلق الانسان»

6- Man achieves the perfection of his creation in the light of learning the Quran. «علّم القرآن، خلق الانسان»

Ayah

عَلَّمَهُ ٱلْبَيَانَ (٤)
[and] taught him articulate speech. (4)

Key Points

Imam Ali عليه السلام says: «لاتجعلنّ ذرب لسانك على من انطقك و بلاغة قولك على من سدّدك»(41) Do not direct the sharpness and harshness of your tongue toward the one who taught you to speak, nor flaunt the eloquence and beauty of your speech before the one who regulated and beautified your discourse. The power of expression and speech is a Divine gift to humanity, and the Quran has provided instructions regarding the manner of expression, including:

It must be well-reasoned and firm. «قولاً سدیداً»(42) It must be appropriate and measured. «قولاً معروفاً»(43) It must be soft and kind. «قولاً لیّناً»(44) It must be eloquent, clear, and explicit. «قولاً بلیغاً»(45) It must be noble and honorable. «قولاً كریماً»(46) It must be beautiful, good, and desirable. «قولوا للنّاس حسناً»(47) The best things should be said. «یقولوا الّتى هى احسن»(48) Even debates and critical discussions must be conducted in the best manner. «جادلهم بالّتى هى احسن»(49)

41) Nahj al-Balaghah, Maxim 411. 42) Nisa, Ayah 9. 43) Baqarah, Ayah 235. 44) Taha, Ayah 44. 45) Nisa, 63. 46) Isra, 23. 47) Baqarah, 83. 48) Isra, 53. 49) Nahl, 125.

Messages

1- Knowledge is the supreme perfection that Allah has bestowed upon humanity. «علّم... علّمه» (Knowledge and teaching are repeated before and after the creation of man.)

2- The specific language or dialect is not important; the fundamental power of expression is what matters. «علّمه البیان»

3- The tongue is the means of expression; the essence of expression is a Divine gift. «علّمه البیان»

4- Parents manifest and actualize the faculty of speech that Allah has embedded within every infant. «علّمه البیان»

5- The creation of man is situated between two Divine teachings. «علّم القرآن، خلق الانسان، علّمه البیان»

6- The power of expression is a manifestation of Divine Mercy. «الرّحمن... علّمه البیان»

7- Those who have the ability to speak multiple languages should recognize their skill as coming from Allah. «علّمه البیان»

8- Knowledge is praiseworthy when it is expressed. «علّم القرآن... علّمه البیان»

Ayahs

ٱلشَّمْسُ وَٱلْقَمَرُ بِحُسْبَانٍ (٥)
The sun and the moon are [disposed] calculatedly, (5)
وَٱلنَّجْمُ وَٱلشَّجَرُ يَسْجُدَانِ (٦)
and the herb and the tree prostrate [to Allah]. (6)

Key Points

Ḥusbān, on the pattern of ghufrān, is a verbal noun meaning "calculation" or "account." The sun and the moon have a calculation (account). Their volume, their weight and mass, their various movements, their orbits, their distance relative to each other and relative to other celestial bodies, the magnitude of their movement, and the extent of their influence upon one another—all of this is calculated. And their calculation is so precise that scientists measure and predict the exact time of lunar eclipses, solar eclipses, and the start of the new year long in advance.

Nujm (نجم) is any growth that does not have a stalk, and shajar (شجر) is a growth that has a stalk. The intent of nujm in this Ayah, based on the context of shajar, is vegetation, not a star in the sky. (50)

Based on the verses of the Quran, the sun, the moon, and the stars have orbits, and all are striving and swimming toward a destination, «كلّ فى فلك یسبحون» (51), and they have a determined lifespan. «كلّ یجرى لاجل مسمّى» (52)

The rotation of night and day and the emergence of seasons is a simple yet precise timekeeping system for all people in all places and times. Islamic acts of worship, such as prayer (Salat) and fasting (Sawm), are regulated based on the rising and setting of the sun and the movement of the moon throughout the year. As we read in the Quran: «أقم الصّلاة لدلوك الشّمس الى غسق الّیل و قرآن الفجر» (53) Establish the prayer from the decline of the sun until the onset of the darkness of the night, and at the time of dawn. «و سبّح بحمد ربّك قبل طلوع الشّمس و قبل غروبها» (54) Glorify and praise Allah before the rising of the sun and after its setting. «ثم اتمّوا الصّیام الى الّیل» (55) And complete the fast until nightfall. And it is stated in narrations: «صم للرّؤیة و افطر للرّؤیة» (56) Begin the fast upon seeing the moon of Ramadan and complete it upon seeing the moon of Shawwal.

Perhaps the intent of the prostration (sujūd) of the vegetation and the tree in the Ayah is their existential submission (khuḍū‘ takwīnī) before the Divine commands, whereby they traverse the path Allah has determined for them without deviation and are submissive to the laws of creation.

50) تفسیر المیزان. 51) انبیاء، 33 و یس، 40. 52) رعد، 2 ؛ فاطر، 13 ؛ زمر، 5 . 53) اسراء، 78. 54) طه، 130. 55) بقره، 187. 56) وسائل، ج 10، ص 257.

Messages

1- The earth and the sky are governed under a single system. «الشّمس والقمر... النّجم والشّجر»

2- Terrestrial vegetation owes its life and growth to the celestial sun and moon. «الشّمس و القمر... النّجم و الشّجر»

3- Existence is submissive to a system predetermined by Allah. «بحُسبان... یسجدان»

Ayahs

وَٱلسَّمَآءَ رَفَعَهَا وَوَضَعَ ٱلْمِيزَانَ (۷)
He raised the heaven high and set up the balance, (7)
أَلَّا تَطْغَوْا۟ فِى ٱلْمِيزَانِ (۸)
declaring, ‘Do not infringe the balance! (8)
وَأَقِيمُوا۟ ٱلْوَزْنَ بِٱلْقِسْطِ وَلَا تُخْسِرُوا۟ ٱلْمِيزَانَ (۹)
Maintain the weights with justice, and do not shorten the balance!’ (9)

Key Points

After mentioning Divine Mercy, the blessing of teaching and articulation, the calculation of the sun and the moon, and the prostration of creatures—which is a form of attention to the relationship between God and humanity and nature—these verses address the manner of the relationship between humanity and society.

Qist (equity) in the Balance (Mizan) means that there should be neither excess demand nor loss and damage.

The Book (Kitab) and the Balance (Mizan) are inseparable. Allah says in Surah Al-Hadid: «و أنزلنا معهم الكتاب و المیزان لیقومَ النّاس بالقسط»(57) In these verses too, both the Quran and the Balance are mentioned: «علّم القرآن... ألاّ تطغوا فى المیزان»

Repetition is a sign of importance. The word Mizan (Balance) is repeated three times in successive verses, indicating the necessity of paying attention to a correct and reliable standard and means of measurement in various matters: «وضع المیزان...», «الاّ تطغوا فى المیزان...» and «لاتخسروا المیزان».

Disregard for the Balance (Mizan) and failure to observe fairness and justice in people's rights lead a person to immorality (fisq and fujur), and immorality leads a person to denial (of truth/disbelief). In Surah Al-Mutaffifin, we read: «ویل للمطفّفین» Woe to the defrauders! Then He says: «اِنّ كتاب الفجّار...» And then: «ویل یومئذ للمكذّبین» It seems that unlawful earnings (luqmah-ye haram) become the prelude to immorality (fujur), and immorality becomes the prelude to disbelief and denial (takdhib).

To establish justice (‘adl) and the Balance (Mizan), Islam has precise and important instructions, to the extent that it is mentioned in narrations: If a judge's shoe is tight, he should not judge while wearing it, lest the pressure exerted on his foot affect his judgment, leading him to haste and ill-temper, and diverting him from a righteous verdict!

Ethics of Trade We read in a hadith: Trade is valuable only if it is based on truth and honesty. «التاجر فاجر و الفاجر فى النّار الاّ مَن أخذ الحق و اعطى الحق»(58)

Imam Ali عليه السلام ordered impure dinars to be thrown into a well so that no transaction could be made with them. «القه فى البالوعه حتّى لایباع شى ءٌ فیه غشٌ»(59)

That Imam used to walk through the market of Kufa every day, sometimes standing in the middle of the market and saying: O group of merchants! «قدّموا الاستخارة», seek goodness from God. «تبرّكوا بالسّهولة», seek blessing through being lenient with people. «جانبوا الكذب», avoid lying in transactions. «و تجانبوا عن الظلم», refrain from oppression and injustice. «و لاتقربوا الربا»(60) and do not approach usury (riba).

57) Hadid, 25. 58) Kafi, vol 5, Adab al-Tijarah. 59) Kafi, vol 5, p 160. 60) Tafsir-e Nemuneh.

Messages

1- The position of the heaven and the earth is not accidental. The raising of the heaven and the volume, size, and distance of the stars are based on wisdom and are a manifestation of Divine Mercy. «الرّحمن... و السماء رفعها و وضع المیزان»

2- Justice is like prayer (Salat) and must be established in society. «و اقیموا الوزن»

3- The Balance (Mizan) and the means of measurement must be just, regardless of what is being weighed. «و اقیموا الوزن بالقسط»

4- The cosmic order is based on the Balance (Mizan). Our lives must also revolve around the Balance. «وضع المیزان ألاّ تطغوا فى المیزان»

5- Justice (‘adl) and the Balance (Mizan) are factors of stability and permanence in the heavens, on the earth, and in society. «وضع المیزان ألاّ تطغوا فى المیزان»

6- Society requires two wings to achieve salvation: knowledge and justice. (In today's world, there is knowledge but no justice.) «علّم القرآن... ألاّ تطغوا فى المیزان»

7- The path to achieving justice is acting upon the Quran. «علّم القرآن... ألاّ تطغوا فى المیزان»

8- In all matters, avoid neither excess «ألاّ تطغوا» nor deficiency. «لاتخسروا»

9- Negligence in services by employees, workers, educators, officials, and religious preachers is also considered defrauding (short-changing the Balance). «لاتخسروا المیزان»

Ayahs

وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَضَعَهَا لِلْأَنَامِ (۱٠)
And the earth—He laid it out for mankind. (10)
فِيهَا فَـٰكِهَةٌ وَٱلنَّخْلُ ذَاتُ ٱلْأَكْمَامِ (۱۱)
In it are fruits and date-palms with sheaths, (11)
وَٱلْحَبُّ ذُو ٱلْعَصْفِ وَٱلرَّيْحَانُ (۱۲)
grain with husk, and fragrant herbs. (12)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (۱۳)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (13)

Key Points

'Anām' means creatures, encompassing jinn, humans, and all beings on Earth. 'Fākihah' refers to any type of fruit, and 'Rayḥān' refers to any fragrant plant. 'Akmām' is the plural of 'Kimm', meaning a covering that envelops the fruit, just as 'Kumm' means a sleeve that covers the hand, and 'Kummah' (on the pattern of qubbah) refers to a cap that covers the head. Perhaps the intent of 'Akmām' in the Ayah is the fibers that surround the date palm, similar to the fibers on a coconut. 'Ḥabb' means seed or grain, and 'Aṣf' means the leaves and parts that separate from the plant, which is why chaff is also called 'Aṣf'. 'Ālā’' is the plural of 'Ilā' meaning blessing or bounty.

The repetition of successive questions accompanied by criticism indicates that every blessing has its own affirmation and denial. In a Hadith, we read that whoever recites the blessed Surah Al-Raḥmān, every time they reach the Ayah «فباىّ آلاءِ ربّكما تكذّبان», they should say: «لابشى ءٍ من آلائك ربّ اُكذّب»(۶۱) (meaning: O my Lord! I do not deny any of Your blessings.)

Discussion on Repetition

The discussion on repetition is presented here due to the first instance of the repeated Ayah «فباىّ آلاءِ ربّكما تكذّبان» in this Surah.

Positive Repetition: Repetition of issues is important and necessary for establishing a culture. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله repeatedly said: «انّى تارك فیكم الثّقلین كتاب اللّه و عترتى» to strengthen the relationship between the Quran and his 'Itrah (Progeny) among the people.

After the revelation of the Ayah «و امر اهلك بالصّلاة»(۶۲), for several months, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله would come to the house of Fatimah Zahra عليها السلام every morning and say: «الصّلاة الصّلاة»(۶۳) to inform the people that his Ahl al-Bayt (Household) are those who are in the house of Ali and Fatimah.

In a Hadith, we read that the reason for repeating «اللّه اكبر» four times at the beginning of the Adhan is to awaken people from heedlessness, but once they are attentive, the remaining phrases are reduced to two repetitions.(۶۴)

Sometimes, Imam Ali عليه السلام would repeat words to create sensitivity among the people. For example, he advised his children: «اللّه اللّه فى الایتام...», «اللّه اللّه فى جیرانكم», «اللّه اللّه فى الصلاة», «اللّه اللّه فى القرآن...»(۶۵), where the majestic word 'Allah' is repeated.

The repetition of prayer (Salat) every day and night is so that we take a step closer to Allah every day. When someone climbs the steps of a ladder, their foot seemingly performs a repetitive action, but in reality, with every movement, they take a step forward and upward. When someone strikes a pickaxe to dig a well, they are seemingly doing a repetitive task, but in reality, with every movement, the depth of the well increases.

With every prayer, remembrance (dhikr), and recitation of an Ayah, a person moves closer to God, until, regarding the Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, we read: «دنى فتدلّى فكان قاب قوسین او ادنى»(۶۶) (He drew near and descended, until He was two bow-lengths away or nearer.)

The weekly repetition of the recommendation for piety (taqwa) in every Friday Prayer sermon demonstrates the role of repetition in the religious education of the people. Fundamentally, the continuation of life relies on repeated breathing, and perfections are achieved through repetition. The permanent trait (malakah) of generosity and courage is not established in a person through a single act of charity or bravery, just as vices and impurities become permanent in the soul through repetition.

Reasons for Repetition in the Quran

Repetition is sometimes for reminding and recalling various blessings. (e.g., the repetition of the Ayah «فباىّ آلاء ربّكما تكذبان»)

Sometimes it is for warning and threatening wrongdoers. (e.g., the repetition of the Ayah «ویل یومئذٍ للمكذّبین»(۶۷))

Sometimes it is for culture building. (e.g., the repetition of «بسم اللّه الرّحمن الرّحیم» at the beginning of every Surah.)

Sometimes it is for establishing conclusive proof (Itmām al-Ḥujjah). (e.g., the repetition of the Ayah «و لقد یسّرنا القرآن للذكر فهل من مدّكر»(۶۸))

Sometimes it is for greater familiarity and internalization. (e.g., the repetition of the Ayah «اذكروا اللّه ذكرا كثیراً»(۶۹))

Sometimes it is for introducing new commands. (e.g., the repetition of «یا ایها الّذین آمنوا..»)

Sometimes it serves as a resolution or conclusion for each segment of a topic or speech. For example, in Surah Ash-Shu'arā', after the report on the work of each prophet concludes, it says: «انّ ربّك لهو العزیز الرحیم»

Sometimes repetition indicates the unity of goal and method. In Surah Ash-Shu'arā', the phrase «فاتقوا اللّه و اطیعون» is repeated many times by various prophets, indicating that their slogan and goal were unified.

۶۱) بحار، ج ۹۲، ص ۳۰۶. ۶۲) طه، ۱۳۲. ۶۳) بحار، ج ۳۵، ص ۲۰۷. ۶۴) علل الشرایع، ج ۱، ص ۲۵۹. ۶۵) نهج البلاغه، نامه ۴۷. ۶۶) نجم، ۹. ۶۷) مرسلات، ۱۵. ۶۸) قمر، ۱۷. ۶۹) احزاب، ۴۱.

Messages

  1. The entire existence has one Administrator, unlike what some polytheists believed, who thought that the heavens and the earth each had a specific administrator. «السّماء رفعها... و الارض وضعها»

  2. The Earth is for the enjoyment and utilization of all earthly creatures, including humans. «وضعها للانام»

  3. The Quran first enumerates spiritual blessings and then material blessings. «علّم القرآن... علّمه البیان... فیها فاكهة و النّخل»

  4. Fruit plays an important role in human nutrition. The name of fruit is mentioned after the creation of the Earth. «والارض... فیها فاكهة»

  5. Among fruits, the value of the date palm is distinct from the rest. «فیها فاكهة... والنّخل»

  6. The husk and even the sheath around plants are blessings. «والحبّ ذو العصف»

  7. The pleasant scent of plants is one of Allah's blessings. «و الریحان»

  8. Denying divine signs leads to reproach. «فباىّ آلاء ربّكما تكذبان»

  9. Jinn, like humans, are responsible, accountable, and benefit from the Earth's blessings. «فباىّ آلاء ربّكما تكذّبان»

  10. Allah created blessings for the comprehensive growth and training of both humans and jinn. «آلاءِ ربّكما»

Ayahs

خَلَقَ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ مِن صَلْصَـٰلٍ كَٱلْفَخَّارِ (۱٤)
He created man out of dry clay, like the potter’s, (14)
وَخَلَقَ ٱلْجَآنَّ مِن مَّارِجٍ مِّن نَّارٍ (۱٥)
and created the jinn out of a flame of a fire. (15)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (۱٦)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (16)

Key Points

Ṣalṣāl means dried clay, Fakhkhār means pottery (baked clay), and Mārij means flame. The Quran uses various expressions regarding the origin of human creation, and these expressions may refer to its different stages: 1. Dust (or Earth). «انّا خلقناكم من تراب»(70) 2. Dust mixed with water. «خلقكم من طین»(71) 3. Foul-smelling clay. «من حماءٍ مسنون»(72) 4. Dried clay. «من صلصال كالفخّار»(73)

The Jinn is an unseen being, and some of its characteristics are mentioned in the Quran, including that the origin of its creation is wind and fire, «خلق الجانّ من مارج من نار», while the origin of human creation is water and earth. «خلق الانسان من صلصال كالفخّار» Its creation preceded the creation of man, «و الجانّ خلقناه من قبل»(74), and like humans, they are believers and disbelievers, male and female, they reproduce and procreate, they possess knowledge, consciousness, and the ability to distinguish right from wrong, and they are capable of performing tasks beyond the capacity of humans. Furthermore, they experience death, resurrection, punishment, and reward.

70) Hajj, 5. 71) An‘ām, 2. 72) Hijr, 28. 73) Ar-Raḥmān, 14. 74) Hijr, 27.

Messages

1- Looking at the past causes human faith and humility to flourish. «خلق الانسان من صلصال...»

2- Allah creates living beings from the cold, lifeless substance of ṣalṣāl and the hot, lifeless substance of mārij. «خلق الانسان... خلق الجان...»

3- The source of human existence is an insignificant and worthless substance. «صلصالٍ» (The indefinite form [of the noun] can be a sign of insignificance.)

4- The Jinn is a real being, not a myth, even if we do not have access to it. «خلق الجانّ من مارج من نار»

5- Jinn and humans are both earthly beings and have been created from material elements. «خلق الانسان من صلصال... خلق الجان من مارج من نار»

6- Denying a blessing is a cause for reproach, regardless of who does it. «فباىّ آلاء ربّكما تكذّبان»

Ayahs

رَبُّ ٱلْمَشْرِقَيْنِ وَرَبُّ ٱلْمَغْرِبَيْنِ (۱۷)
Lord of the two easts, and Lord of the two wests! (17)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (۱۸)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (18)

Key Points

Although the sun has a sunrise and a sunset every day of the year, considering the ascending arc and the descending arc of its movement, it actually has two Easts (Mishraq) and two Wests (Maghrib), and the rest are between these two. This system causes the emergence of different seasons and the change in the hours of day and night throughout the year. However, some commentators have considered the two Easts (al-Mashriqayn) and the two Wests (al-Maghribayn) to be the rising and setting of the moon and the sun, respectively.(75) Imam Ali عليه السلام, in a sermon after reciting the Ayah «ربّ المشرقَین...» (Lord of the two Easts...), said: The East of winter is in its own boundary, and the East of summer is in its own boundary.(76)

75) Tafsir Namuneh. 76) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Allah's encompassment of the East and West of the universe is uniform. «ربّ المشرقَین... ربّ المغربَین»

2- The Quran invites humanity to cosmology. «المشرقین... المغربین»

3- The Quran draws humanity's attention to the knowledge of both the horizons (afaq) and the souls (anfus). «خلق الانسان من صلصال... ربّ المشرقَین و... المغربَین»

4- Natural phenomena are blessings both for humans and for Jinn. «ربّ المشرقَین و ربّ المغربَین فباىّ آلاء ربّكما»

5- The movement of the Earth is such that the East and West are created, and this is among the divine blessings. «فباىّ آلاء ربّكما تكذّبان»

Ayahs

مَرَجَ ٱلْبَحْرَيْنِ يَلْتَقِيَانِ (۱۹)
He merged the two seas, meeting each other. (19)
بَيْنَهُمَا بَرْزَخٌ لَّا يَبْغِيَانِ (۲٠)
There is a barrier between them, which they do not overstep. (20)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (۲۱)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (21)
يَخْرُجُ مِنْهُمَا ٱللُّؤْلُؤُ وَٱلْمَرْجَانُ (۲۲)
From them emerge the pearl and the coral. (22)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (۲۳)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (23)
وَلَهُ ٱلْجَوَارِ ٱلْمُنشَـَٔاتُ فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ كَٱلْأَعْلَـٰمِ (۲٤)
His are the sailing ships on the sea [appearing] like landmarks. (24)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (۲٥)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (25)

Key Points

Maraj means to let loose (or set free), and Barzakh refers to the barrier or dividing line between two things. Lu'lu' means a pearl that grows inside a shell, and Marjan refers both to small pearls and to a type of marine animal resembling a tree branch that fishermen catch. Bahr refers to a large body of water, whether it is a sea or a river, and the intent of Bahrain (the two seas) is the salty and sweet waters. Usually, river water is sweet (fresh) and sea water is salty. As He says in other verses: «مایستوى البحران هذا عذبٌ فرات سائغ شرابه و هذا مِلح اُجاج»(77) The two seas are not alike; one has water that is pleasant, sweet, and delightful to drink, and the other is salty and bitter. Salty water is for preserving the life of marine creatures, and sweet water is for preserving the life of plants, vegetation, humans, and animals that live on land.

The phrase «مرَج البحرین» (He has set free the two seas) appears twice in the Quran: once in Surah Al-Furqan, Ayah 53, and once in this Surah (Ar-Rahman). There (in Al-Furqan), He first speaks of the great struggle (Jihad) against the disbelievers: «لاتطع الكافرین و جاهدهم به جهاداً كبیراً» (Do not obey the disbelievers, but strive against them with a great striving), and following that, He says: «مرج البحرین هذا عذب فرات و هذا مِلحٌ اُجاج جَعَل بینهما برزخاً و حِجراً محجورا» (He has set free the two seas, one sweet and palatable, and the other salty and bitter, and He has placed between them a barrier and an insurmountable partition). But in this Surah (Ar-Rahman), the discussion first concerns the Balance, accountability, and justice: «وضع المیزان - اقیموا الوزن - لاتخسروا المیزان» (He established the Balance - Observe the weight with justice - Do not defraud the Balance), and after that, He says: «مرج البحرین یلتقیان . بینهما برزخ لا یبغیان» (He has set free the two seas meeting together. Between them is a barrier, so neither of them transgresses). Meaning, the salty and sweet waters do not transgress against each other, because non-transgression is more consistent with justice. Following that, He says: «یخرج منهما اللؤلؤ و المرجان» (From both of them come forth pearls and coral). Yes, if there is justice and no transgression, beauties emerge.

The religion of Islam is a natural religion (Din Fitri), and Allah has provided for the innate inclinations and needs of humanity, one of which is the inclination toward beauty and adornment. This is exemplified by a precious gem like the pearl, which is extracted from the sea. In describing other blessings, the Quran did not use the word lahu (for Him), but regarding the ships, He said: «و له الجوار» (And His are the ships). Perhaps this is to indicate that even what is made by your hands is from Him, because your thought, design, physical strength for construction, and the materials and their effects are all from Him. When Imam Ali عليه السلام recited these verses: «یخرج منهما اللؤلؤ و المرجان», he was asked: "Then why do you not use these blessings?" He replied: Allah has made it obligatory upon just leaders to live their lives so simply and austerely that poverty does not become difficult for the poor. (78) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, in interpreting the verse «مرج البحرین یلتقیان», said: «علىّ و فاطمةعلیهما السلام بحران عمیقان، لایبغى احدهما على صاحبه، یخرج منهما اللؤلؤ و المرجان، الحسن و الحسین علیهما السلام» (Ali and Fatimah علیهما السلام are two deep seas, neither of which transgresses against the other, and from these two seas emerge pearls and coral, namely Hasan and Husayn علیهما السلام). (79)

77) Fatir, 12. 78) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa'iq. 79) Tafsir Qummi.

Messages

1- Existence is a classroom of Monotheism (Tawhid); the sky, the earth, and its sea. «و السّماء رفعها... والارض وضعها... مرج البحرین...»

2- The sea is a blessing for all humans and jinn, even though not everyone lives near the sea. «مرج البحرین... فباىّ آلاء ربّكما»

3- The signs of Allah's power and the supremacy of His Will over everything are evident. The salty sea water meets and encounters the sweet sea water; they pass alongside each other but do not mix. «بینهما برزخ لایبغیان»

4- Utilizing blessings requires effort and striving. «یخرج منهما اللؤلؤ...»

5- Water and earth are the foundation and source for fulfilling human needs. «فیها فاكهة و النخل... و الحب ذو العصف...یخرج منهما اللؤلؤ و المرجان»

6- The role of the sea in transporting passengers and cargo, as a free, ready, vast, and expansive passage, has long been noted by humans, and Allah draws man's attention to it in these verses. «الجوار المنشئات فى البحر كالاعلام»

7- The blessing of the sea and its creations is among the great blessings for which Allah reproaches the denier. The phrase «فباىّ آلاء ربّكما تكذّبان» (Then which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?) is repeated three times in these verses.

Ayahs

كُلُّ مَنْ عَلَيْهَا فَانٍ (۲٦)
Everyone on it is ephemeral, (26)
وَيَبْقَىٰ وَجْهُ رَبِّكَ ذُو ٱلْجَلَـٰلِ وَٱلْإِكْرَامِ (۲۷)
yet lasting is the majestic and munificent Face of your Lord. (27)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (۲۸)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (28)

Key Points

We read in Ayah 185 of Surah Aal-i-Imran: «كلّ نفس ذائقة الموت» (Every soul shall taste death). We also read in the last Ayah of Surah Al-Qasas: «كلّ شى ء هالك الاّ وجهه» (Everything will perish except His Face/Essence of the Lord). In this Surah, too, we read: «كلّ من علیها فان و یبقى وجه ربّك» (Everyone upon it [the earth] will perish, and there will remain the Face of your Lord). In the preceding verses, the blessings were introduced. In this Ayah, He says: the time for benefiting from these blessings is short; seize the opportunity, for you are all transient. Although all beings are perishable, whoever is on the Divine path and whose work takes on a Divine color—that pure intention, which is the "Face of the Lord" (وجه ربّ), makes his perishable work enduring.

Messages

1- Death is a general and comprehensive law. «كلّ مَن علیها فان»

2- Do not rely on anyone but Allah, for all are perishable. «كلّ من علیها فان ویبقى وجه ربّك»

3- The value of man lies in proximity to Allah, not in possessing resources. «كلّ مَن علیها فان و یبقى وجه ربّك»

4- Death is a step in the path of human development. «كلّ مَن علیها فان و یبقى وجه ربّك»

5- The Prophet is under the special nurturing and Lordship (Rububiyyah) of Allah. «ربّك»

6- The death of beings is not a sign of deficiency or the exhaustion of Divine power and generosity, for He is the Possessor of Majesty and Honor (Jalal and Ikram). «كلّ من علیها فان و یبقى... ذوالجلال و الاكرام»

7- Divine Majesty (Jalal) is accompanied by mercy and compassion (Ra'fat). «ذوالجلال و الاكرام»

8- Death is a blessing (Ni'mah). «كلّ من علیها فان... فباىّ آلاء ربّكما» (Although no material blessing was mentioned in Ayahs 26 and 27, the phrase «فباىّ آلاء...» is repeated again in Ayah 28; therefore, death is also a blessing.)

9- Jinn, like humans, experience death. This is because the address «ربّكما» refers back to both Jinn and mankind. «كلّ من علیها فان... فباىّ آلاء ربّكما»

Ayahs

يَسْـَٔلُهُۥ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ هُوَ فِى شَأْنٍ (۲۹)
Everyone in the heavens and the earth asks Him. Every day He is engaged in some work. (29)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (۳٠)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (30)

Key Points

Asking and requesting is sometimes through the language of state (حال) and sometimes through the language of speech (قال). Whether man expresses his need for Allah or not, in any case, he is always in need of God. The meaning of "يوم" (day) in the Ayah is not the literal day, but absolute time, because Allah is not contained within time; rather, He is beyond time. Regarding the interpretation of the Ayah «كلّ یوم هو فى شأن» (Every day He is engaged in [some] affair), we read in a Hadith: Allah creates a new matter every day that did not exist before. «من احداث بدیع لم یكن» (80) Imam Ali عليه السلام, concerning the Ayah «كلّ یوم هو شأن», said: Every day He forgives a sin, removes a sorrow, raises a group, and lowers another group. (81)

(80) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā'iq. (81) Tafsir Burhān.

Messages

1- Angels and celestial beings, just like the inhabitants of the earth, extend their hands of need toward Allah. «یسئله من فى السموات و الارض»

2- Divine actions are not imitative, but rather innovative and original. «كلّ یوم هو فى شأن»

3- The constant need of creation necessitates the constant grace and bounty of Allah. (He possesses new and continuous bounty for His creatures every moment.) «یسئله... كلّ یوم هو فى شأن»

4- It is not the case that Allah created the universe and then abandoned it; rather, He constantly manages and administers the affairs of existence. «كلّ یوم هو فى شأن»

Ayahs

سَنَفْرُغُ لَكُمْ أَيُّهَ ٱلثَّقَلَانِ (۳۱)
Soon We shall make Ourselves unoccupied for you, O you notable two! (31)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (۳۲)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (32)

Key Points

The meaning of sanfaghu (سنفرغ) is not leisure after occupation, but rather special attention and complete dedication to an important matter. If the word 'leisure' (faraaghat) is used in relation to Allah's accountability, then a judge must also free himself from all other matters when passing judgment. Yes, just adjudication requires attention, precision, and freedom of thought and mind.

According to the author of Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan, since jinn and humans have superiority over other creatures and possess intellect, the power of choice, and discernment, they are referred to as thiql (ثِقل). "O you two weighty ones" (Ayyuha ath-Thaqalan).

The Reckoner on the Day of Judgment is Allah. "And sufficient are We as reckoners" (كفى بنا حاسبین)(82). The reckoning is swift. "And He is the swiftest of reckoners" (و هو أسرع الحاسبین)(83).

Imam Ali عليه السلام was asked: How does Allah reckon with all creation? He replied: In the same way that He provides sustenance for all of them (84). Everyone will be questioned, even the Prophets. "Then We will surely question those to whom [messengers] were sent, and We will surely question the messengers" (فلنسئلنّ الّذین أرسل ألیهم و لنسئلنّ المرسلین)(85).

The reckoning for some is easy. "An easy reckoning" (حساباً یسیراً)(86). The reckoning for some is difficult. "They fear the evil of the reckoning" (یخافون سوء الحساب)(87), "A severe reckoning" (حساباً شدیداً)(88).

We read in a hadith: The type and manner of the servants' reckoning depends on the gifts and divine favors granted [to them]: "Indeed, Allah scrutinizes the servants in the reckoning on the Day of Resurrection according to the measure of intellect He granted them in the world" (انّما یداق اللّه العباد فى الحساب یوم القیامة على قدر ما آتاهم من العقول فى الدنیا)(89). Verily, the degree of precision in the reckoning of the servants on the Day of Resurrection will be according to the measure of understanding and intellect given to them in this world.

It is stated in a narration: Those who are strict and narrow-minded in their dealings and transactions with people will have a difficult and severe reckoning (90).

We read in narrations: If you reckon with yourselves in this world and hold yourselves accountable, Allah will make it easy for you on the Day of Resurrection. And Imam Kadhim عليه السلام said: He is not one of us who is heedless of himself in this world and does not attend to the reckoning of his deeds (91).

82) Al-Anbiya, 47. 83) Al-An'am, 62. 84) Nahj al-Balaghah, Maxim 300. 85) Al-A'raf, 6. 86) Al-Inshiqaq, 8. 87) Ar-Ra'd, 21. 88) At-Talaq, 8. 89) Al-Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 11. 90) Bihar, Vol. 7, p. 266. 91) Wasa'il, Vol. 11, Chapter on Self-Reckoning.

Messages

1- Accountability is among the affairs of management and nurturing (tarbiyah). "The Face of your Lord... We will attend to you..." (وجه ربّك... سنفرغ لكم...)

2- Accountability will be held for all material and spiritual blessings. From the beginning of the Surah until now, various material and spiritual blessings have been mentioned, and in this Ayah, He says: Soon We will attend to your reckoning: "We will attend to you, O you two weighty ones" (سنفرغ لكم ایّه الثقلان).

3- Everyone must be prepared for accountability. "We will attend to you, O you two weighty ones" (سنفرغ لكم ایّه الثقلان).

4- Allah is engaged in a matter and affair every moment; today He grants, and tomorrow He reckons. "Every day He is in [some] affair. We will attend to you" (كل یوم هو فى شأن سنفرغ لكم).

5- Allah mobilizes all resources for the reckoning. (The use of nafraghu (نفرغ) instead of afraghu (افرغ) may be an indication of this matter.)

6- Although nothing distracts Him from another matter, "No affair distracts Him from another affair" (لایشغله شأن عن شأن), yet to show the importance of the reckoning, He comes to the accountability "free" (faarighan). "We will attend to you" (سنفرغ لكم).

7- Jinn and humans are both responsible (mukallaf) and subject to accountability. "O you two weighty ones" (ایّه الثقلان).

8- Belief in Allah's accountability affects how we utilize worldly blessings. "We will attend to you, O you two weighty ones. So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?" (سنفرغ لكم ایّه الثقلان. فباىّ آلاء ربّكما تكذبان).

Ayahs

يَـٰمَعْشَرَ ٱلْجِنِّ وَٱلْإِنسِ إِنِ ٱسْتَطَعْتُمْ أَن تَنفُذُوا۟ مِنْ أَقْطَارِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ فَٱنفُذُوا۟ ۚ لَا تَنفُذُونَ إِلَّا بِسُلْطَـٰنٍ (۳۳)
O company of jinn and humans! If you can pass through the confines of the heavens and the earth, then do pass through. But you will not pass through except by an authority [from Allah]. (33)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (۳٤)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (34)

Key Points

The word Ma'shar (مَعشر) means group, derived from the root ‘Ashr (عَشر), meaning the number ten. The word ‘Ashīrah (عشیرة), meaning relatives, is also derived from this root. The word Ma'shar is used three times in the Qur’an. Sultān (سلطان) refers both to coercive and material power, which causes dominance over others, and to intellectual power, proof, evidence, and firm argumentation, which causes intellectual and scientific mastery. "و لقد أرسلنا موسى بآیاتنا و سلطان مبین" (92), "یجادلون فى آیات اللّه بغیر سلطان" (93) Ayah 33 may relate to this world and discussions of space travel, passing through the Earth's atmosphere, and penetrating the heavens, or, based on the context of the preceding and succeeding verses, it may relate to the Day of Judgment and the impossibility of escaping the Divine court. The depths of the Earth, the heights of space, and the celestial spheres are conquerable by humans and jinn. The expression "ان استطعتم" (if you are able), instead of "لو استطعتم" (if you were able), indicates possibility, and the phrase "الاّ بسلطان" (except by authority/power) is the key that one can penetrate the heavens and the Earth. Especially since the word Sultān is usually used in the Qur’an for scientific mastery.

92) Ghafir, 23. 93) Ghafir, 35.

Messages

1- Jinn and humans will be addressed by Allah together on the Day of Judgment. "یا معشر الجنّ و الانس..."

2- Attention to Divine power and human helplessness prevents deviation. "لاتنفذون..."

3- Subtle beings like the jinn also lack the ability to escape the Divine court. "یا معشر الجنّ... لاتنفذون"

4- Escape from punishment on the Day of Judgment is not possible for either jinn or humans except by possessing rational or legal proof and evidence. "یا معشر الجنّ و الانس... لاتنفذون الاّ بسلطان"

Ayahs

يُرْسَلُ عَلَيْكُمَا شُوَاظٌ مِّن نَّارٍ وَنُحَاسٌ فَلَا تَنتَصِرَانِ (۳٥)
There will be unleashed upon you a flash of fire and a smoke; then you will not be able to help one another. (35)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (۳٦)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (36)

Key Points

Shuwāẓ means massive and terrifying flames of fire, and nuḥās means molten and glowing copper. The Day of Judgment is depicted in these verses as follows: On the one hand, Allah will conduct the reckoning with special attention. «سنفرغ لكم» On the other hand, man has no way to escape. «ان استطعتم ان تنفذوا... فانفذوا» On the other hand, various types of torment will pour down from every direction. «یرسل علیكم شواظ... و نحاس» On the other hand, no one will be able to seek help. «فلاتنتصران» The one who did not benefit from the sending of the Prophets, «یرسل رسولا»(94), and the one who did not properly utilize the sending of material blessings, «یرسل السماء علیكم مدرارا»(95), «یرسل الریاح بشرا»(96), such a person must await the sending of torment with molten copper. «یرسل علیكما شواظ من نار و نحاس»

94) Shura, 51. 95) Hud, 52. 96) Naml, 63.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, fire and molten copper will prevent the deniers from escaping. «یرسل علیكما شواظ من نار و نحاس فلاتنتصران»

2- Even the Jinn, despite their origin being fire, are vulnerable to fire just like humans. «یرسل علیكما شواظ من نار»

3- Awareness of future torments is a blessing, as it causes one to distance oneself from sin. «فباىّ آلاء ربّكما تكذّبان»

Ayahs

فَإِذَا ٱنشَقَّتِ ٱلسَّمَآءُ فَكَانَتْ وَرْدَةً كَٱلدِّهَانِ (۳۷)
When the sky is split open, and turns crimson like tanned leather. (37)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (۳۸)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (38)
فَيَوْمَئِذٍ لَّا يُسْـَٔلُ عَن ذَنۢبِهِۦٓ إِنسٌ وَلَا جَآنٌّ (۳۹)
On that day neither humans will be questioned about their sins nor jinn. (39)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (٤٠)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (40)

Key Points

There are several possibilities regarding the meaning of "لایسئل" (He will not be questioned/asked):

  1. Lā yus'alu 'an dhanbi al-mujrim (He will not be questioned about the sin of the criminal): No one else, neither human nor jinn, will be held accountable in place of the criminal; rather, the criminal himself will be held accountable. (97)
  2. We read in narrations that the Day of Judgment has fifty stations (98). In one station, the command is for detention, reckoning, and questioning: "و قفوهم انّهم مسؤلون" (99). In another station, seals are placed upon the lips: "نختم على افواههم" (100). In yet another station, they seek help from one another, and in another, everyone is weeping. Therefore, if we read in these verses that no one will be questioned, it pertains to a specific station.
  3. It is possible that the intent of the verse, "لایسئل عن ذنبه" (He will not be questioned about his sin), is that only the sin itself will not be questioned, because the sin of the transgressor will be present on that Day: "و وجدوا ما عملوا حاضرا" (101), and his inner secrets will be revealed: "تبلى السرائر" (102). Therefore, questions will be asked about other matters, such as lifespan, income, and how it was spent, and not solely about human sins.

In the future, the heavens will split apart and melt. The heavens, which are "سبعاً شدادا" (seven strong ones) (103), will flow like molten oil: "فكانت وَردة كالدّهان".

97) Majma' al-Bayan. 98) Bihar, Vol. 7, p. 126. 99) As-Saffat, 24. 100) Ya Sin, 65. 101) Al-Kahf, 49. 102) At-Tariq, 9. 103) An-Naba', 12.

Messages

1- The advent of the Day of Judgment occurs through a transformation in the natural order. "فاذا انشّقت السماء..."

2- The end of this world is collapse and dissolution, but it is the beginning of the next world. "فاذا انشّقت السّماء... فیومئذ..."

3- Jinn, just like humans, possess free will and commit sins. "لایسئل عن ذنبه انس و لا جانّ"

Ayahs

يُعْرَفُ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ بِسِيمَـٰهُمْ فَيُؤْخَذُ بِٱلنَّوَٰصِى وَٱلْأَقْدَامِ (٤۱)
The guilty will be recognized by their mark; so they will be seized by their forelocks and feet. (41)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (٤۲)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (42)

Key Points

Sīmā (countenance/sign) is derived from sūm, meaning an apparent sign or mark. Nawāṣī is the plural of nāṣiyah, meaning the hair at the front of the head. Apparently, this verse is the answer to a question that could be raised following the previous verse: If no questioning occurs regarding sin, then how will the criminals be recognized? This verse answers by stating: «یعرف المجرمون بسیماهم» (The criminals will be recognized by their countenances.) (104) Of course, just as the wicked are recognized by their countenances, the righteous are also recognized by theirs. «تعرف فى وجوههم نضرة النعیم» (You will recognize in their faces the freshness of bliss.) (105) Countenances on the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah): Those whose faces are bright. «تبیضّ وجوه» (106) Those whose faces are dark. «وجوههم مسودّة» (107) Faces that are joyful. «وجوه یومئذ ناضرة» (108), «وجوه یومئذ ناعمة» (109) Faces that are grim and sorrowful. «وجوه یومئذ باسرة» (110) Faces that are laughing. «وجوه یومئذ مسفرة . ضاحكة مستبشرة» (111) Faces covered with dust and grime. «وجوه یومئذ علیها غبرة» (112) Faces that are humbled and disgraced. «وجوه یومئذٍ خاشعه» (113)

104) Tafsir al-Mizan. 105) Mutaffifin, 24. 106) Āl ‘Imrān, 106. 107) Zumar, 60. 108) Qiyāmah, 22. 109) Ghāshiyah, 8. 110) Qiyāmah, 24. 111) ‘Abasa, 38 and 39. 112) ‘Abasa, 40. 113) Ghāshiyah, 2.

Messages

1- A person's thoughts and deeds are manifested on his face. «یعرف المجرمون بسیماهم»

2- The criminal, in the utmost humiliation, will be seized by Divine wrath. «فیؤخذ بالنواصى و الاقدام»

3- Those who spared no sin in this world and acted contrary to the Divine command from the hair of their head to the tips of their toes, will also be seized by the hair of their head and cast into torment on the Day of Judgment. «فیؤخذ بالنواصى و الاقدام»

4- The separation of the good from the wicked and their recognition is one of Allah's blessings. «فباىّ آلاء ربّكما تكذّبان»

Ayahs

هَـٰذِهِۦ جَهَنَّمُ ٱلَّتِى يُكَذِّبُ بِهَا ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ (٤۳)
‘This is the hell which the guilty would deny!’ (43)
يَطُوفُونَ بَيْنَهَا وَبَيْنَ حَمِيمٍ ءَانٍ (٤٤)
They shall circuit between it and boiling hot water. (44)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (٤٥)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (45)

Key Points

Ḥamīm means hot water, and the word ḥammām (bathhouse) is cognate with it. Ān means a liquid that is extremely scorching. Denying Paradise and Hell, and disbelieving in the Day of Judgment, are among the factors that lead a person to Hellfire. Just as in Sūrat Al-Muddaththir, one of the reasons for entering Hell is stated as the denial of the Day of Judgment: «ما سلككم فى سقر، قالوا لم نك من المصلّین... و كنّا نكذّب بیوم الدین»

The people of Paradise are the center of the circumambulation (ṭawāf), and the servants of Paradise circle around them with the vessels of Paradise; «یطوف علیهم ولدان مخلّدون»(114) Immortal youths (in splendor and freshness) continuously circle around them. «یطاف علیهم بصحاف من ذهب واكواب»(115) Golden dishes of food and goblets (of pure drinks) are circulated around them. «یطاف علیهم بكأس من معین»(116) Overflowing cups are circulated around them. «یطاف علیهم بآنیة من فضّة و أكواب كانت قواریرا»(117) Silver vessels and crystal goblets are circulated around them. But the inhabitants of Hell must humiliatingly circle around Hell and the scorching water. «یطوفون بینها و بین حمیم آن»

114) Al-Wāqi‘ah, 17. 115) Az-Zukhruf, 71. 116) As-Sāffāt, 45. 117) Al-Insān, 15.

Messages

1- The criminals will see the recompense for what they continuously denied. «هذه جهنّم»

2- Crime and sin are the basis for denying the Day of Judgment. «یكذّب بها المجرمون»

3- The inhabitants of Hell circle around the torment (due to intense fear, terror, thirst, and searching for an escape route). «یطوفون بینها و بین حمیم آن»

4- Divine Justice and the punishment of the criminal are among the blessings of Allah. «فباىّ آلاء ربّكما تكذّبان»

Ayahs

وَلِمَنْ خَافَ مَقَامَ رَبِّهِۦ جَنَّتَانِ (٤٦)
For him who stands in awe of his Lord will be two gardens. (46)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (٤۷)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (47)
ذَوَاتَآ أَفْنَانٍ (٤۸)
Both abounding in branches. (48)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (٤۹)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (49)
فِيهِمَا عَيْنَانِ تَجْرِيَانِ (٥٠)
In both of them will be two flowing springs. (50)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (٥۱)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (51)
فِيهِمَا مِن كُلِّ فَـٰكِهَةٍ زَوْجَانِ (٥۲)
In both of them will be two kinds of every fruit. (52)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (٥۳)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (53)

Key Points

Afnaan (أفنان) is either the plural of fanan (فنن), meaning a fresh branch, or the plural of faann (فانّ), meaning types/varieties. In the fortieth section of the Du'a Jawshan Kabir, we read: "O Best of the Beloveds" (یا خیر المحبوبین). Allah is the best Beloved, so our fear is of His station (Maqam), not His essence (Dhat); [it is the fear of] His station of awareness, presence, justice, and encompassment.

Fear of the Divine station is a factor in preventing sin and attaining Paradise, just as we read in Ayah 40 of Surah An-Nazi'at: «و أمّا مَن خاف مقام ربّه و نهى النّفس عن الهوى فانّ الجنّة هى المأوى» (But as for him who feared the station of his Lord and restrained the soul from [low] desire, then surely Paradise will be the abode).

The source of fear must be the ma'rifah (gnosis) and recognition of the Lord's station, «خاف مقام ربّه» (feared the station of his Lord), just as tears are valuable when they are based on recognition. «تفیض من الدمع ممّا عرفوا من الحق»(118) means that they shed tears because of the recognition and gnosis they attained.

In Surah Al-Insan, concerning the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام, we read that they gave their food for three consecutive days to the orphan, the needy, and the captive, saying: We fear the Lord and the Day of Judgment, «انّا نخاف من ربّنا یوما عبوساً قمطریرا»(119). And in this Ayah [of Surah Ar-Rahman] we read: «و لمن خاف مقام ربّه جنّتان» (And for him who fears the station of his Lord are two Gardens). Therefore, the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام are certainly destined for Paradise.

It is stated in narrations that whoever feared Allah in this world will be safe in the Hereafter, and whoever felt secure in this world will be in fear in the Hereafter.(120)

Imam Sadiq علیه السلام said: «من علم انّ اللّه یراه و یسمع ما یقول من خیر و شر فیحجزه ذلك عن القبیح من الاعمال فذلك الّذى خاف مقام ربّه و نهى النفس عن الهوى»(121) (Whoever knows that Allah sees him and hears whatever he says, whether good or evil, and this prevents him from ugly deeds, then that is the one who feared the station of his Lord and restrained the soul from [low] desire).

In a Hadith, we read that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله told Imam Ali علیه السلام: Several things lead to salvation, one of which is the fear of Allah in secret and in public.(122)

A close relationship with a divine scholar ('alim Rabbani) strengthens the fear of Allah's station. In a Hadith, we read: «مَن مَشى فى طلب العلم خُطوتَین و جَلس عند العالم ساعتین و سَمِع من المعلّم كلمتین اوجب اللّه له جنّتین كما قال: «و لمن خاف مقام ربّه جنّتان»»(123) (Whoever walks two steps in pursuit of knowledge, sits with a scholar for two hours, and learns two words from a teacher, Allah makes two Gardens obligatory for him, just as He said: «و لمن خاف مقام ربّه جنّتان»). He then cited this Ayah of the Quran.

Fear of Allah's station in this world is the means of remaining safe from all fears in the Hereafter, just as this phrase is repeated in other verses of the Quran: «ألا انّ اولیاء اللّه لا خوف علیهم و لا هم یحزنون»(124).

The fruits of Paradise require neither effort to pick, «جَنى الجنّتین دان» (the harvest of the two Gardens is near), nor does accessing them take time, «قطوفها دانیة»(125) (its clusters hanging low), nor are they limited, «فاكهة كثیرة»(126) (abundant fruit), nor are they seasonal. «لامقطوعة و لاممنوعة»(127) (neither interrupted nor forbidden).

Luqman advised his son: Have such fear of Allah that the worship of jinn and humans does not make you complacent, and have such hope in Allah's favor that the sins of the two worlds (mankind and jinn) do not make you despair. A similar statement has also been narrated from Hadrat Ali علیه السلام.(128)

Imam Baqir علیه السلام narrates from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله that no one should rely on his own deeds, but rather rely only on the favor and mercy of Allah.(129)

118) Ma'idah, 83. 119) Insan, 10. 120) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa'iq. 121) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 122) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa'iq. 123) Irshad al-Qulub, vol. 1, p. 195. 124) Yunus, 62. 125) Haqqah, 23. 126) Waqi'ah, 32. 127) Waqi'ah, 33. 128) Arba'in Imam Khomeini, 221. 129) Arba'in Imam Khomeini, 226.

Messages

1- In the court of Divine Justice, race, color, occupation, age, and gender are not the criteria for evaluation. «ولمن خاف... جنّتان»

2- The fear of God is the source of accepting the truth and performing righteous deeds; therefore, no other condition besides fear is mentioned for entering Paradise. «و لمن خاف... جنّتان»

3- The Essence (Dhat) of Allah is the source of mercy. Human attention to the station and position of Allah in the cosmic order and the court of the Day of Judgment leads to precision in behavior and speech and prevents the occurrence of crime and sin. «خاف مقام ربّه»

4- In education (or moral training), threat and encouragement must be side by side. « هذه جهنّم... جنّتان»

5- In Paradise, there is abundance, «من كلّ فاكهة» (of every fruit), diversity, «زوجان» (two kinds), and accessibility. «جنى الجنتین دان» (the harvest of the two Gardens is near).

Ayahs

مُتَّكِـِٔينَ عَلَىٰ فُرُشٍۭ بَطَآئِنُهَا مِنْ إِسْتَبْرَقٍ ۚ وَجَنَى ٱلْجَنَّتَيْنِ دَانٍ (٥٤)
[They will be] reclining on beds lined with green silk. And the fruit of the two gardens will be near at hand. (54)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (٥٥)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (55)
فِيهِنَّ قَـٰصِرَٰتُ ٱلطَّرْفِ لَمْ يَطْمِثْهُنَّ إِنسٌ قَبْلَهُمْ وَلَا جَآنٌّ (٥٦)
In them are maidens of restrained glances, whom no human has touched before, nor jinn. (56)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (٥۷)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (57)
كَأَنَّهُنَّ ٱلْيَاقُوتُ وَٱلْمَرْجَانُ (٥۸)
As though they were rubies and corals. (58)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (٥۹)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (59)

Key Points

Baṭā'in is the plural of biṭānah, meaning lining or inner covering, and istabraq is thick silk (brocade). Janā means the picking of fruit, and dān means being near and accessible (within reach). Qāṣirāt aṭ-ṭarf either means not casting their eyes upon others, or that because of their attractiveness and beauty, their husbands are unwilling to take their eyes off them and look at others.

In these verses, the material and natural enjoyments of Paradise are mentioned: 1. Dwelling in gardens, orchards, and beside springs. « جنّتان، افنان، عینان» 2. Benefiting from all kinds of foods and fruits. «كلّ فاكهة زوجان» 3. Complete comfort and tranquility. «متّكئین» 4. Pure and beautiful spouses.(130) «قاصرات الطرف»

The women of Paradise are both chaste and virtuous, «قاصرات الطرف», and virgin and untouched, «لم یطمثهنّ...», and also delicate and beautiful. «كانهنّ الیاقوت و المرجان»

(130) Tafsir Kabir of Fakhr al-Razi.

Messages

  1. The fear of today is the tranquility of tomorrow. «و لمن خاف... متكئین على فرش...»

  2. The people of Paradise possess sexual desires. «فیهن قاصرات الطرف»

  3. Jinn also possess sexual instinct. «لم یطمثهنّ قبلهم انس و لاجان»

  4. Man's inclination toward the purity, virginity, and beauty of his spouse is an inherent desire, not an induced inclination born of culture and environment. «لم یطمثهنّ قبلهم...»

  5. The women of Paradise are distinct from the spouses of the people of Paradise in this world, because no one has touched them. «لم یطمثهنّ قبلهم»

  6. First the chastity of the woman, then her beauty. First «لم یطمثهن» and then «كانهنّ الیاقوت»

Ayahs

هَلْ جَزَآءُ ٱلْإِحْسَـٰنِ إِلَّا ٱلْإِحْسَـٰنُ (٦٠)
Is the requital of goodness anything but goodness? (60)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (٦۱)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (61)

Key Points

Ihsan (احسان) has two meanings: one is good work (a good deed), and the other is kindness to others. The meaning of the first Ihsan in the Ayah refers to good work, and the meaning of the second Ihsan refers to kindness toward others. (131) Regarding «هل جزاء الاحسان الاّ الاحسان» (Is the recompense of goodness anything but goodness?), Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The phrase «هل جزاء الاحسان...» applies to both the disbeliever and the believer, and whoever performs a good deed for another, it is incumbent upon the latter to compensate him. (132)

In the Quran, the principle of reciprocity has been repeated many times: «فاذكرونى أذكركم» (133) Remember Me, and I will remember you. «اِن عُدتم عُدنا» (134) If you return, We will return. «فاعتدوا علیه بمثل ما اعتدى علیكم» (135) Then transgress against him to the same extent that he transgressed against you. «و لهنّ مثل الّذى علیهنّ» (136) And for them (women) are rights similar to those (duties) upon them. «اِن عاقبتم فعاقبوا بمثل ما عوقبتم» (137) If you punish [an enemy], then punish with the like of that with which you were afflicted. (If you intend to take revenge on the enemy, strike a blow equal to the damage you suffered.) «اِن تنصروا اللّه ینصركم» (138) If you support Allah, He will support you. «فلمّا زاغوا أزاغ اللّه» (139) So when they deviated, Allah caused their hearts to deviate. (If they deviate by their own will, Allah leaves them in deviation.)

Imam Ali عليه السلام narrates from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله that Allah said: Is the reward of the one upon whom I have bestowed the blessing of Monotheism (Tawhid) anything but Paradise? (140) «هل جزاء مَن أنعمتُ علیه بالتّوحید الاّ الجنّة»

131) Tafsir Rahnama. 132) Tafasir Majma‘ al-Bayan and Al-Mizan. 133) Al-Baqarah, 152. 134) Al-Isra, 8. 135) Al-Baqarah, 194. 136) Al-Baqarah, 228. 137) An-Nahl, 126. 138) Muhammad, 7. 139) As-Saff, 5. 140) Amali Tusi, Vol. 2, p. 182.

Messages

1- In return for the Ihsan (goodness) and kindness of others, we must also be kind. «هل جزاء الاحسان الا الاحسان» (Is anything deserved in return for all this Ihsan from Allah to man, except the goodness of the servant?)

2- We should only think about values, not individuals, gender, race, age, region, or tribe. «هل جزاء الاحسان الا الاحسان»

3- The reward for Ihsan, regardless of its type, amount, time, place, or the person involved, must be Ihsan. «هل جزاء الاحسان الا الاحسان»

4- One of the philosophies behind the Day of Judgment is the implementation of justice and the recompense for acts of Ihsan. «هل جزاء الاحسان الاّ الاحسان»

5- Allah’s good reward for our good deeds is a blessing. «فباىّ آلاء ربّكما تكذّبان»

Ayahs

وَمِن دُونِهِمَا جَنَّتَانِ (٦۲)
Beside these two, there will be two [other] gardens. (62)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (٦۳)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (63)
مُدْهَآمَّتَانِ (٦٤)
Dark green. (64)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (٦٥)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (65)
فِيهِمَا عَيْنَانِ نَضَّاخَتَانِ (٦٦)
In both of them will be two gushing springs. (66)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (٦۷)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (67)

Key Points

"Dūn" (دون) sometimes means 'other than' and sometimes means 'lower.' Therefore, two meanings have been proposed for the Ayah, «و من دونهما جنّتان» (And besides these two, there are two Gardens): A) Besides those two Gardens, there are two other Gardens. B) For the friends of Allah, there are those two Gardens mentioned: «و لمن خاف... جنتان» (But for him who fears... two Gardens), but for ordinary believers, there are two other Gardens which are lower than the Gardens of the friends of Allah. (141)

"Mudhāmmatah" (مدهامّة) is from the root "Daham" (دَهَم) and the pattern "Idhimām" (ادهیمام), meaning intense blackness. Here, it refers to a garden and orchard that appears black due to the intensity of its greenness.

"Nadhdhākh" (نضّاخ) means gushing and bubbling.

It is mentioned in a Hadith that water, greenery, and a beautiful face bring vitality and freshness. (142) The poet has rendered this Hadith into verse:

ثلاثةٌ یُذهبن عن قلب الحَزن الماءُ و الخَضراءُ و الوجهُ الحَسن

Three things remove sorrow and grief from the heart: water, greenery, and a beautiful countenance.

In these Ayahs, all three items are mentioned among the blessings of Paradise: Water: «عینان نضّاختان» Greenery: «مدهامّتان» And beautiful faces: «كانهنّ الیاقوت و المرجان»

141) Tafsir Burhan. 142) Bihar, Vol. 59, p. 144.

Messages

1- The trees of Paradise are always lush and fresh. «مدهامّتان» (The color green is beneficial for refreshing the environment.)

2- The Quran pays attention to the color green. «مدهامّتان»

3- The best pleasures are the pleasure of observing natural scenery. «عینان نضّاختان»

4- Water is one blessing, and its gushing is another blessing. «عینان نضّاختان»

5 - The gushing of springs, «نضّاختان», along with the flow of rivers, «تجرى من تحتها الانهار», and the existence of waterfalls, «و ماء مسكوب», and the variety of rivers of milk, honey, wine, and water, are indescribable blessings. «فباىّ آلاء ربّكما تكذّبان»

Ayahs

فِيهِمَا فَـٰكِهَةٌ وَنَخْلٌ وَرُمَّانٌ (٦۸)
In both of them will be fruits, date-palms and pomegranates. (68)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (٦۹)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (69)
فِيهِنَّ خَيْرَٰتٌ حِسَانٌ (۷٠)
In them are maidens good and lovely. (70)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (۷۱)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (71)
حُورٌ مَّقْصُورَٰتٌ فِى ٱلْخِيَامِ (۷۲)
Houris secluded in pavilions. (72)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (۷۳)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (73)
لَمْ يَطْمِثْهُنَّ إِنسٌ قَبْلَهُمْ وَلَا جَآنٌّ (۷٤)
Whom no human has touched before, nor jinn. (74)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (۷٥)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (75)
مُتَّكِـِٔينَ عَلَىٰ رَفْرَفٍ خُضْرٍ وَعَبْقَرِىٍّ حِسَانٍ (۷٦)
Reclining on green cushions and lovely carpets. (76)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ (۷۷)
So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (77)
تَبَـٰرَكَ ٱسْمُ رَبِّكَ ذِى ٱلْجَلَـٰلِ وَٱلْإِكْرَامِ (۷۸)
Blessed is the Name of your Lord, the Majestic and the Munificent! (78)

Key Points

"Rafraf" (رَفرف) is a type of green cloth that looks like a lush garden. "Abqariyy" (عَبقرىّ) originally means a place specific to the Jinn, unknown to the public. It was then applied to anything rare and uncommon, and here it refers to rare and uncommon carpets.

Among the fruits of Paradise, dates and pomegranates hold a special place, and thus, alongside the general title of "fākiha" (فاكهة), which includes all kinds of fruit, these two fruits are mentioned separately.

Worldly enjoyments are usually accompanied by heedlessness and sin, but in Paradise, such deficiencies never exist. The characteristics of the spouses in Paradise are inner and outer beauty in exclusive places, "Khayrāt Hisān" (خیرات حسان), and appropriate covering, "Hūr Maqsūrāt fī al-Khiyām" (حور مقصورات فى الخیام).

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: The women of Paradise possess good morals and beautiful faces. "Khayrāt al-Akhlāq Hisān al-Wujūh" (143)

"Hūr Maqsūrāt" (حور مقصورات) are different from "Khayrāt Hisān" (خیرات حسان). The phrase "lam yaṭmithhunna" (لم یطمثهن) is for the "Hūr Maqsūrāt," not for the "Khayrāt Hisān." This is because no human or jinn has had intercourse with the houris, but the Khayrāt Hisān are worldly women. We read in narrations: The "Khayrāt Hisān" are the righteous women of this world who will be more beautiful than the Hūr al-'Īn (houris) on the Day of Judgment (144).

There are differences between the two categories of gardens mentioned in this Sūrah, including: A) In the first two gardens, even the inner lining of the couches is silk, "baṭā'inuhā min istabraq" (بطائنها من استبرق), but in the second two gardens, only the surface of the fabrics is beautiful and colorful. "Rafraf" (رفرف). B) In the two gardens for the pious (muttaqīn), there were two types of every fruit, "fīhimā min kulli fākihatin zawjān" (فیهما من كلّ فاكهة زوجان), but in the two gardens for ordinary believers, only the existence of fruit is mentioned. "fīhimā fākiha" (فیهما فاكهة). C) Regarding the spouses in the first two gardens, the expression "qāṣirāt aṭ-ṭarf" (قاصرات الطرف) is used, and in the second two gardens, "maqsūrāt fī al-khiyām" (مقصورات فى الخیام). D) What is identical in all four gardens is the virginity of the spouses. In both cases, it is stated: "lam yaṭmithhunna insun qablahum wa lā jānn" (لم یطمثهنّ انس قبلهم و لاجان).

Throughout the entire Qur'an, the phrase "Dhū al-Jalāli wa al-Ikrām" (ذوالجلال و الاكرام) appears twice, both times in this very Sūrah.

The Sūrah begins with "Raḥmān" (رحمن) and ends with "Ikrām" (اكرام), meaning the source of all those blessings is the Mercy and Generosity of Allah.

143) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn. 144) Kāfī, Vol. 8, p. 156.

Messages

1- Sometimes encouragement must be achieved through things that are familiar, well-known, and beloved to man. "fākiha wa nakhil wa rummān" (فاكهة و نخل و رمّان)

2- Specific individuals and items must be named separately. Alongside the general title of fruit, the names of dates and pomegranates are mentioned separately. "wa nakhil wa rummān" (و نخل و رمّان)

3- The women of Paradise are both good in character ("Khayrāt" - خیرات) and beautiful in appearance ("Hisān" - حسان).

4- Modesty and covering are a value, even in Paradise. "Hūr Maqsūrāt fī al-Khiyām" (حور مقصورات فى الخیام)

5- Allah is the source of all goodness and blessings, and seeking blessing through His blessed Name is appropriate. "Tabāraka ismu Rabbika" (تبارك اسم ربّك)

6- All blessings, beauties, punishments, and rewards are manifestations of Allah's Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). "Tabāraka ismu Rabbika" (تبارك اسم ربّك)

7- The Majesty and Glory of Allah are accompanied by Honor and Generosity. "Dhū al-Jalāli wa al-Ikrām" (ذوالجلال و الاكرام)
